title: Define Keyboard Shortcuts for Menu Items
breadcrumbs: Define Keyboard Shortcuts for Menu Items
text: Define Keyboard Shortcuts for Menu Items Menu definitions can define keyboard shortcuts that enable the user to select a menu item using the keyboard rather than the mouse. Keyboard shortcuts can be defined as an access key or an accelerator key . Access key—a letter in a menu name, such as F in the File menu. By default, the first letter in a menu option is the access key. If that letter has already been used by a previous menu item, a different letter is automatically used. To define a different access key edit the menu option Title and place a percent sign ( % ) before the character to use as the access key. Accelerator key—a key combination, such as Alt+O to open a file. The key combination is displayed next to the menu item. To define an accelerator key: In the Accelerator property of the menu item, enter a logical name for the menu sequence or action, such as FileOpen . Note: The maximum length of a logical name is 30 characters. In the [accelerators] section of the application's .ini file, assign the desired keyboard sequence to this logical name. For example, FileOpen=Alt+O Related Links [accelerators] The [accelerators] section of the initialization file enables you to map a logical function, such as fileopen to a keyboard sequence (an accelerator) such as Ctrl+F7.
